name: Buster Harding
description: Canadian-born American jazz pianist, composer and arranger
abstract: Lavere "Buster" Harding (March 19, 1917 – November 14, 1965) was a Canadian-born American jazz pianist, composer and arranger.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Buster Harding was my main man for arranging, and a lot of times he'd play piano in the band

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Then pretty soon he got Buster Harding, and Buster stayed with him all through the things... he always liked to have an arranger, because like you're doing that picture... we did the -- a little after, 'The Sensations of 1944,' with Eleanor Powell and Woody Herman.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Buster Harding, and the interviewee confirms he was arranging for Cab Calloway, but the interviewee does not claim a personal or professional relationship with Harding himself.

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: Count Basie had been through here and had given us several beautiful scores by Hugo Winterhalter, Buster Harding, and several other arrangers.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Buster Harding was arranger and second or stand-in pianist. The only ones in this band that were not new to me were... and of course, Teddy.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: the young arrangers began to appear on the scene like Tad Dameron, Buster Harding… But I think Buster Harding did more writing for the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
